allTasks.description: Set up OpenWeatherMap free API integration in weatherService.ts. Add support for VITE_WEATHER_API_KEY environment variable. Implement the real fetchCurrentWeather, fetchForecast, and geocodeLocation functions using OpenWeatherMap API endpoints: 1) Current Weather (https://api.openweathermap.org/data/2.5/weather), 2) 7-day Forecast (https://api.openweathermap.org/data/2.5/forecast?appid=YOUR_KEY&units=metric/imperial), 3) Geocoding (https://api.openweathermap.org/geo/1.0/direct). Keep existing mockData fallback functions active for testing without API key. Export a new function getSavedLocation() and saveLocation(lat, lon, name) that persists to localStorage.

Create location persistence hook and integrate with LocationPicker. Create src/hooks/useLocation.ts that exports: useLocation() returning [currentLocation, setCurrentLocation, isLoading]. Should read from localStorage key 'weather-app-location' on mount, include functions to load/save zip code or coordinates. Update LocationPicker component to accept currentLocation prop and show it as the default value in input field when open. When user enters new location/zip, save immediately via useSettings context.

Update WeatherHero and StatsGrid components to match reference layout from references/main.html. Requirements: 1) Main temperature display must be centered, dominant (text-7xl on mobile, text-9xl on desktop), use gradient background purple-800 to black for depth. 2) Temperature should animate when changing - add bounce/scale effect using framer-motion AnimatePresence. 3) Weather icon below temp (lucide-react icons mapped from OpenWeatherMap codes). 4) Condition text large and bold below icon. 5) StatsGrid cards should use rounded-xl, semi-transparent bg-black/40 with backdrop-blur-sm, subtle shadow-lg, gold/yellow accent for values when 'good' conditions, red for warnings.

Update BottomNav component to match game-like aesthetic from references. Requirements: 1) Fixed position bottom (fixed bottom-0 left-0 right-0). 2) Background gradient bg-gradient-to-t from-black/95 to-black/80 with backdrop-blur-md. 3) 4 navigation buttons minimum - Home, Forecast, Radar, Settings using lucide-react icons (Home, CloudSun, Map, Settings). 4) Each button press should scale down 0.92 then animate back up (framer-motion whileTap={{ scale: 0.92 }}). 5) Active nav item gets gold/amber-400 glow with text-amber-400 and bg-amber-400/20 background. 6) Touch targets minimum 48px height for mobile accessibility.

Update index.css for global styles and Tailwind layer setup. Add @tailwind base, components, utilities directives properly organized. Set default font family (sans-serif Inter/system-ui). Define CSS variables for game-like color scheme in :root --primary-gold: #F59E0B, --danger-red: #EF4444, --success-green: #10B981, --dark-bg: #0A0A0A. Add smooth transitions globally (transition-all duration-200 ease-out). Set body background to dark gradient from-zinc-950 via-purple-950/30 to-black. Ensure text defaults are white/text-white with proper contrast ratios.

Update tailwind.config.js with custom theme extensions matching the game aesthetic. Add extend section with: colors (gameGold, dangerRed, successGreen, deepBlack), animation keyframes for 'pulse-glow' (box-shadow glow effect on hover/active states) and 'bounce-in' (scale from 0.9 to 1). Add fontFamily config using sans-serif default. Configure corePlugins to enable preflight. Set content paths properly ['index.html', './src/**/*.{js,ts,jsx,tsx}'].

Implement ForecastRow component updates for proper forecast display. Each forecast day should: 1) Show date (Mon, Tue format) in bold text-lg at left. 2) Weather icon in center using getWeatherIconClass from weatherService. 3) High/Low temps displayed as '85° / 62°' style on right side with amber color for high, blue-gray for low. 4) Row height minimum 60px for touch targets. 5) Horizontal scroll container (w-full overflow-x-auto) for multiple days. Use the ForecastRow existing interface but add animation - each row should have subtle slide-in effect when appearing using framer-motion stagger.

Update App component to integrate location hook and weather fetching flow. Wire useLocation() hook into App state. When currentLocation changes, trigger refetch of weather data via weatherService functions. Add loading states with spinner animation using framer-motion. On initial load, check for saved location - if exists fetch immediately, otherwise show LocationPicker modal by default. Update Header component to display current city name from Location context. Add error handling for API failures that falls back to mockData.ts.

global._researchDocs: ## UI/UX Design Principles for Web Applications ### Layout - Main interaction element must be centered and visually dominant (40-60% of viewport) - Use vertical single-column layouts for game/app UIs — never side-by-side grids unless it's a dashboard - Content hierarchy: hero/main action → stats/feedback → secondary actions (shop, settings) - Mobile-first: everything should work on a 375px wide screen and scale up - Scrollable secondary content (shops, lists) should never push the main interaction off-screen ### Visual Feedback - Every user interaction (click, purchase, hover) MUST produce visible feedback - Number changes should animate (bounce, scale pulse, color flash) - Buttons: press animation (scale 0.95), hover glow/lift, disabled state with reduced opacity - Success actions: green flash, checkmark, particle burst - Use CSS transitions (200-300ms) on all interactive elements ### Color & Contrast - Dark backgrounds with bright accent colors for maximum contrast - Use gradients over flat colors for depth (e.g., purple-900 to indigo-950) - Interactive elements should be the brightest items on screen - Text must have sufficient contrast — white/yellow on dark, with text-shadow for readability ### Typography - Big, bold numbers for scores/stats (text-4xl to text-6xl) - Clear hierarchy: title (bold, large) → subtitle (medium) → body (regular, smaller) - Monospace or tabular numbers for counters that change frequently ### Animation - Idle animations (slow pulse, float, rotate) make the UI feel alive - Click animations should be fast (100-200ms) and snappy - Spawn/death animations for appearing/disappearing elements - Use CSS will-change on animated elements for performance ### Cards & Containers - Rounded corners (border-radius: 12-16px) - Subtle shadows for depth (shadow-lg, shadow-xl) - Semi-transparent backgrounds with backdrop-blur for overlay panels - Hover: lift (translateY -2 to -4px) + shadow increase --- ## Library Documentation (from Exa Web Search) ## Docs: framer-motion Temperature AnimatePresence Weather usage examples Title: GitHub - Simple examples for animation, gestures, components, transforms and more. ## #Animation The`animate`prop can accept an object of values. When one --- ## Docs: lucide-react AnimatePresence Weather OpenWeatherMap usage examples Title: Lucide for React – Lucide URL: https://lucide.dev/guide/packages/lucide-react Code/Highlights: Lucide for React ​ Lucide provides a React component library for using icons in your applications. Each icon is available as a standalone component that renders an optimized inline SVG. List of features: - Easy to use – Import icons and use them directly in JSX. - Customizable – Adjust size, color, stroke width, and other properties via props. - Tree-shakable – Only the icons you import are included in your final bundle. - TypeScript support – Fully typed components for a better developer experience. ## Overview ​ Getting startedLearn how to get started with Lucide for React. Migration from v0Learn how to migrate from v0 to v1 of Lucide. Migration from React FeatherLearn how to migrate from `react-feather` to `lucide-react` ### Basics ​ ColorAdjust the color of your icons SizingAdjust the size of your icons Stroke widthAdjust the stroke width of your icons ### Advanced ​ TypescriptAll exported types and how to use them AccessibilityMaking your icons accessible Global stylingApply global styles to all icons With Lucide LabUsing lucide-lab with lucide-react Filled iconsUsing filled icons in lucide-react Aliased NamesUsing aliased icon names Combining iconsCombine multiple icons into one Dynamic icon componentDynamically import icons as needed ### Resources ​ Accessibility in depthAccessibility best practices VSCodeVSCode and Lucide --- Title: Getting started - React – Lucide URL: https://lucide.dev/guide/react/getting-started Code/Highlights: started ​ This guide will help you get started with Lucide in your React project. Make sure you have a React environment set up. If you don't have one yet, you can create a new React project using Create React App, Vite, or any other React ... ## Installation ​ pnpmyarnnpmbun sh ``` pnpm add lucide-react@next ``` sh ``` yarn add lucide-react@next ``` sh ``` npm install lucide-react@next ``` sh ``` bun add lucide-react@next ``` ## Importing your first icon ​ Lucide is built with ES Modules, so it's completely tree-shakable. Each icon can be imported as a React component, which renders an inline SVG element. This way, only the icons that are imported into your project are included in the final bundle. The rest of the icons are tree-shaken away. jsx ``` import { Camera } from 'lucide-react'; // Usage const App = () => { return ; }; export default App; ... ## Props ​ To customize the appearance of an icon, you can use the following props: | name | type | default | | --- | --- | --- | | `size` | number | 24 | | `color` | string | currentColor | | `strokeWidth` | number | 2 | | `absoluteStrokeWidth` | boolean | false | Because icons render as SVG elements, all standard SVG attributes can also be applied as props. See the list of SVG Presentation Attributes on MDN. jsx ``` // Usage const App = () => { return ; }; ``` More examples and details how to use props, continue the guide: ColorAdjust the color of your icons [SizingAdjust the size of your icons]< --- Title: AspiringSaint/weather-app-1 URL: https://github.com/AspiringSaint/weather-app-1 Code/Highlights: Open issues: 0 - Primary language: JavaScript - Languages: JavaScript (57.2%), CSS (38.3%), HTML (4.5%) - Default branch: main - Homepage: https://weather-app-1-delta.vercel.app/ - Created: 2025-09-19T00:42:24Z - Last push: 2025-09-19T00:50:28Z - Contributors: 1 (top: AspiringSaint) --- # 🌤 Weather App (React + Open-Meteo) A simple, mobile-friendly weather application built with **React**, **Open-Meteo API** (no API key required), and **lucide-react icons**. The app allows users to search for any city and view the current temperature, wind speed, and weather condition with a dynamically changing background. --- ## 🚀 Features - **City Search:** Type in a city name and fetch its current weather. - **Dynamic Background:** Background color changes based on weather conditions (sunny, cloudy, rainy, snowy). - **Responsive Design:** Mobile-friendly with adaptive layout for smaller screens. - **Modern UI:** Glassmorphism-inspired card design with smooth transitions. - **Error Handling:** Displays messages for invalid cities or failed API calls. --- ## 🛠️ Tech Stack - **React** (functional components + hooks) - **Axios** (for API calls) - **Lucide-react** (for icons) - **Open-Meteo API** (free weather + geocoding data) - **CSS3** (for styling & responsiveness) --- ## 📦 Installation Clone this repository and install dependencies: ```bash git clone https://github.com/your-username/weather-app.git cd --- Title: lucide-react - npm URL: https://www.npmjs.com/package/lucide-react Code/Highlights: Dependencies] - 11051 Dependents - 649 Versions Lucide icon library for React applications. About· Icons· Documentation· License # Lucide React Implementation of the lucide icon library for React applications. ## Installation ``` pnpm add lucide-react ``` ``` npm install lucide-react ``` ``` yarn add lucide-react ``` ``` bun add lucide-react ``` ## Documentation For full documentation, visit lucide.dev ## Community Join the Discord server to chat with the maintainers and other users. ## License Lucide is licensed under the ISC license. See LICENSE. ## Sponsors ### Awesome backers 🍺 ### Backers ☕ ### Other contributors 💸 You can find all our past and non-recurring financial contributors at our Open Collective page. ## Readme ### Keywords - Lucide - React - Feather - Icons - Icon - SVG - Feather Icons - Fontawesome - Font Awesome Viewing lucide-react version 0.577.0 --- Title: Weather Card | Free & Open Source Animated ReactJS Components URL: https://animata.design/docs/widget/weather-card Code/Highlights: Weather Card | Free & Open Source Animated ReactJS Components Docs Components Blog Star us on GitHub Docs # Weather Card A cool weather widget ## Installation ### Install dependencies `bash npm
